channel.title: AI on SensorLab — Jozef Stefan Institute
channel.description: Recent content in AI on SensorLab — Jozef Stefan Institute

item.title: 6G-OPTICON: 6G Optimization and Constrained Intelligence for End-to-End Network Operations across Heterogeneous Segments
item.description: 6G-OPTICON is designing a next-generation, end-to-end orchestration framework tailored for the 6G era. By embedding constraint-native deep learning throughout the orchestration pipeline, the project enables intelligent, adaptive planning, optimization, and management across a complex landscape of interconnected networks and heterogeneous domains spanning O-RAN, core, transport, and edge.

item.title: AcceptRemote: Acceptance of Remote Operations for Future Mobility
item.description: AcceptRemote places Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH) at the core of its design, ensuring that technological innovations in remote operations are co-created with and for society. It develops a Societal Readiness Framework and demonstrates remote operations through three pilots — RideCare, LogiCare and FlexiCare — combining AI-powered predictive communication, generative scenario tools, and multi-agent fleet coordination.

item.title: GENOME: GENerative and connected intelligence for 6G Open ManagemEnt
item.description: AI and large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to handle complex tasks across many fields. In network management, they offer powerful tools to automate operations, improve efficiency, and optimise performance. Supported by the Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions programme, the GENOME project aims to design, develop, and test LLM-based solutions for the autonomous management of different network domains.

item.title: MNEME: A Collaborative Memory Platform for Cultural Heritage
item.description: MNEME is a large-scale, user-driven validation of the European Collaborative Cloud for Cultural Heritage (ECCCH). Through five pilots in Greece, Spain, Slovenia, Italy and France, the project harmonises curated assets (texts, images, 3D models) into a federated knowledge graph that enables cross-collection discovery, comparative analysis and shared storytelling across institutions.
